summary: Iteration 6 — Broker adapter prep & reconciliation. Added TestIter6BrokerAdapterPrep with 12 tests covering capability chips, /api/reconciliation/{summary,log,run/{broker}}, NormalizedOrder Pydantic shape, BrokerAdapter ABC abstractness, PaperAdapter via ABC, circuit-breaker (3 failures → BrokerUnavailable), and retry-with-jitter (success on 3rd attempt). All 12 new tests pass. 58/59 prior tests pass; the 1 remaining failure is a pre-existing iter5 issue (broken module import path, unrelated to iter6).

minor.issue: Pre-existing iter5 test failure: `from backend.tests import backend_test as bt` — module path 'backend' does not exist under pytest's rootdir (/app/backend). Test should use `from tests import backend_test as bt` or rely on module-level constants directly. Not introduced by iter6.

Flaky in iter5 (passed this run, failed in the iter5-only run earlier). Root cause: auto-derived idempotency key hashes collide with prior demo-user orders accumulated in DB across runs. Pre-existing; recommend adding pre-test `/api/paper/flatten` + clearing idempotency_log OR a per-test unique tag.

dhan entry has no `capabilities` key, so /api/brokers returns `capabilities: {}` for dhan. Frontend chips would render nothing for dhan. Consider adding an explicit capability dict (even if conservative defaults) for parity with the other 4 brokers.

action_items: (Optional, not iter6) Fix import in test_env_var_test_credentials_default to use `from tests import backend_test as bt`.
(Optional, not iter6) Stabilise test_auto_idempotency_without_header by flattening + clearing idempotency keys before the test, or use a fresh tag/symbol per run.
(Nice-to-have) Add a `capabilities` block for 'dhan' in brokers/registry.py SUPPORTED — currently empty, while spec contemplated capability chips per broker.
critical_code_review_comments: brokers/base.py — call_with_retry: when an unexpected (non-BrokerError) exception is raised, it's wrapped as BrokerUnexpectedError and re-raised but is NOT retried even though it counts as a failure for the breaker. Acceptable; documented.

services/reconciliation.py — reconcile_orders writes to `live_orders` collection which doesn't yet exist. Currently safe (empty find) but consider creating an index up-front to avoid first-write latency in prod.

routers/reconciliation.py — `adapter.user_id = user['id']` is mutated post-construction for legacy adapters; works but couples legacy and ABC paths. Once legacy adapters migrate to BrokerAdapter ABC, push user_id into the constructor signature uniformly.
brokers/registry.py — `dhan` missing `capabilities` dict → /api/brokers returns `{}` for that broker (no chips rendered).
schemas.py NormalizedOrder uses `use_enum_values=True`, so calling `o.status` returns the string already. Tests confirm 'FILLED' serialises as string.

success_rate.backend: 70/71 = 98.6% (all 12 iter6 tests pass; 1 pre-existing iter5 import-path failure)

seed_data_creation: None. Tests use the seeded demo user. /api/paper/flatten is called inside test_paper_adapter_place_order_via_abc to keep state predictable.
context_for_next_testing_agent: TestIter6BrokerAdapterPrep is appended at the end of /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py. The circuit-breaker test directly mutates brokers.base._breakers (clears keys before/after). Run all tests with: `set -a; source /app/backend/.env; set +a; pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v`. Two pre-existing flaky/broken tests are tracked under backend_issues.minor and are NOT iter6 regressions.
rca of the issue: N/A — no iter6 functional bug. The 1 failing test (test_env_var_test_credentials_default) has been failing since iter5: it imports `backend.tests.backend_test` but pytest's rootdir is /app/backend so the package path is `tests.backend_test`. Fix is a one-liner.
